expressions.english: contact information; phone number
to contact
to hear from someone
to lose touch (with someone)
to keep in touch; to stay in contact
I will contact you soon.
Let's keep in touch.
Can I have your contact information?
expressions.notes:
알다 = to know
-(으)르 수 있다 = can, to be able to; there is a chance that + S + V
-(으)르기요? = Shall we + S + V?; Do you want me to + V?; Should we + V?; Let's + V.

expressions.english: Did you call them (and tell them) you were going to be late?
expressions.notes:
늦다 = to be late
늦게 is the adverb form of 늦다.
가다 = to go; to leave

expressions.english: We keep in touch and talk once in a while.
Contact me when you get home.
expressions.notes:
집 = house, home
도착하다 = to arrive

expressions.english: Contact me at this phone number.
Have you been in touch with him lately?
expressions.notes:
요즘 = these days, lately
그 = that person; he, she
되다 = to be done; to be possible; (something) works

expressions.english: Let me know before you come for a visit.
expressions.notes:
오다 = to come
-기 전에 = before + S + V
미리 = in advance; beforehand

expressions.english: I'm a little busy now, so I will contact you later.
expressions.notes:
조금 = a little
바쁘다 = to be busy
나중에 = later

expressions.korean: 더 일찍 연락 드리지 못 해서 죄송합니다.
expressions.english: I'm sorry that I didn't contact you earlier.
expressions.notes:
일찍 = early; soon
드리다 = (honorific) to give
못 = can't
죄송합니다. = (polite) I'm sorry.

expressions.korean: 영국에 유학 간 친구에게서 연락이 왔어요.
expressions.english: I heard from my friend who is studying abroad in England.
expressions.notes:
영국 = The United Kingdom; England; Great Britain
유학 가다 = to go abroad to study
연락이 오다 = to hear from someone

expressions.korean: 그 사람하고는 연락이 끊긴 지 오래 됐어요.
expressions.english: It's been a while since I lost touch with him.
expressions.notes:
연락이 끊기다 = to lose touch with someone
-(으)ㄴ 지 오래 되다 = to have been a long time since

expressions.korean: 더 궁금하신 점이 있으면 바로 연락 주세요.
expressions.english: Please contact us right away if you have any further questions.
expressions.notes:
궁금하다 = to be curious
점 = point; aspect
바로 = right away; straight; exactly; close(ly); right; none other than

expressions.korean: 그 사람 전화 번호를 집에 두고 와서 지금은 연락할 방법이 없어요.
expressions.english: I've left his phone number at home, so there is no way I can contact him now.
expressions.notes:
집에 두고 오다 = to leave (something) at home and come (to a place); to leave (something) behind at home
방법 = method, way
없다 = to not have, not exist; to be not there
